steps: The line of best fit has two key features to read from the scatterplot: the y-intercept (where the line crosses the y-axis) and the slope (direction and steepness).
The line crosses the y-axis at approximately \(y = 2.8\), so the y-intercept is positive (+2.8). This eliminates choices C (\(-2.8\) intercept) and D (\(-2.8\) intercept).
As \(x\) increases, \(y\) also increases, so the slope is positive (+1.7). This eliminates choice B (slope \(-1.7\)).
The equation \(y = 2.8 + 1.7x\) has a positive y-intercept of 2.8 and a positive slope of 1.7, matching the line shown.
choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — '\(y=2.8+1.7x\)': Positive y-intercept (2.8) and positive slope (1.7) — matches a line that rises from left to right and crosses the y-axis above the origin. ✓
  • Choice B — '\(y=2.8-1.7x\)': Positive y-intercept but negative slope — the line would fall from left to right, contradicting the upward trend shown. ✗
  • Choice C — '\(y=-2.8+1.7x\)': Negative y-intercept — the line would cross the y-axis below the origin, contradicting the scatterplot. ✗
  • Choice D — '\(y=-2.8-1.7x\)': Both the intercept and slope are negative — the line falls and starts below the origin, contradicting the scatterplot. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
